title: Everyday Scaries — Discovery Pre-Work
cover_lede: Complete this before your Discovery session and return it at least 48 hours in advance.
howto_title: Six sections to shape a new content brand
howto_paras: Your answers give your Blink strategist the context they need to make the Discovery session focused and actionable — not generic. The session itself is where we go deep; the pre-work is how we get there faster.

Most questions take 1–2 minutes and only need your honest instinct. A few ask you to look something up first — your analytics, channels, or team info. Those are marked. Pull that data before you start so you're not hunting for it mid-form.
est_time_html: Estimated time: ~25 minutes  ·  including a few quick data lookups
ready_label: Before you start — have these ready

ready.a: Any audience you can already reach
ready.b: LinkedIn following, email list, community — even if small
other creators or brands in and around this space
who creates content, how many hours per week are available

sections.title: Your Brand & Message
sections.subtitle: What you stand for, who it's for, and where you're headed

questions.name: q1_1
questions.text: What is Everyday Scaries about, and what is its promise to the people who follow it?
questions.help: Think of it as your one-line pitch — why should someone follow Everyday Scaries instead of scrolling past? Focus on the message and what it gives people (permission, perspective, solidarity), not who it's for yet — we'll cover that in Section 03.

questions.name: q1_2
questions.text: Where does Everyday Scaries live today, and where do you want it to be in 12 months?
questions.help: You've started on LinkedIn. List the platforms you'll build on — LinkedIn, a newsletter, a podcast, Instagram, etc. — and note what's already started versus planned.

questions.name: q1_3
questions.text: What are your top 1–2 priorities for Everyday Scaries over the next 12 months?
questions.help: E.g. establishing the brand and its identity, growing a following, building audience affinity and trust, launching a subscription newsletter, planning a podcast.

questions.name: q1_4
questions.text: What do you want your content to do most right now?
questions.help: E.g. build awareness and recognition for the brand, grow a following, deepen audience affinity and trust, drive newsletter sign-ups, seed a community, establish your voice on the topic.

sections.title: Your Brand Voice
sections.subtitle: How you talk and what you stand for

questions.name: q2_1
questions.text: Describe the voice and personality of Everyday Scaries in 3–5 words or phrases.
questions.help: Don't overthink it. First instinct is usually most accurate.

questions.name: q2_2
questions.text: What do you believe about entrepreneurship and fear that most voices in this space aren't saying?
questions.help: This is your POV — the conviction that makes Everyday Scaries different from typical founder or hustle content. E.g. “The fear isn't a flaw, it's proof you care.” or “We glorify the grind and ignore the 3am doubt.”

sections.title: Your Audience
sections.subtitle: Who follows you — and what makes it resonate

questions.name: q3_1
questions.text: Describe the person Everyday Scaries is for. Who are they, and where do they already spend their attention?
questions.help: Include where they are in their journey (aspiring, early-stage, scaling), what they are feeling, and where they consume content — LinkedIn, newsletters, podcasts, communities, etc.

questions.name: q3_2
questions.text: What is the #1 reason someone would follow Everyday Scaries and stay?
questions.help: The specific feeling or belief that makes it resonate — being seen, feeling less alone, gaining perspective. This becomes the core of your content strategy.

sections.title: Your Space
sections.subtitle: Whose voices share it — and what room they leave for you

questions.name: q4_1
questions.text: Name 3–5 creators, brands, or voices your audience already follows in and around this space.
questions.help: Include both direct voices (others talking about the founder journey, fear, or mental health) and content they follow for inspiration. These don't have to be the same.

questions.name: q4_2
questions.text: What are those voices NOT saying that your audience clearly wants more of?
questions.help: Think honesty about fear, the emotional side of building, real stories over highlight reels, practical perspective, community — what's missing from how this space talks to people?

sections.title: Your Content & Capacity
sections.subtitle: What you've built and what you can realistically produce

questions.name: q5_1
questions.text: What have you published so far, and where? Include follower or connection counts if you have them.
questions.help: You've started on LinkedIn. List: platform → posts per week (approx.) → current following. This is pre-brand, so rough numbers are fine — or write that there is nothing formal yet.

questions.name: q5_2
questions.text: Has any post resonated noticeably — comments, shares, DMs, saves? What was it, and why do you think it landed?
questions.help: Check your LinkedIn analytics for standout posts. If it is still early, describe the kind of content you've seen resonate for similar voices.

questions.name: q5_3
questions.text: Who will create the content — and how many hours per week are realistically available?
questions.help: List who's involved: you, a collaborator, a freelancer, a ghostwriter or editor. Be honest about actual availability, not what you wish it were.

questions.name: q5_4
questions.text: What content formats feel most natural or exciting — and which do you want to avoid?
questions.help: E.g. personal essays and short posts, talking-to-camera or short-form video, storytelling from your own experience, interviews, a newsletter, a podcast, quote graphics. Be honest about what you'll actually sustain.

sections.title: Your Goals for Discovery
sections.subtitle: What you want to walk away with

questions.name: q6_1
questions.text: What is the single most important thing you want to leave the Discovery session with?
questions.help: E.g. a clear content strategy, a channel decision, a positioning statement, an audience definition, a content calendar framework, clarity on what to actually post.

questions.name: q6_2
questions.text: Is there anything about you, the brand, or where it's headed that we haven't asked about — that you think we need to know going in?
questions.help: Optional. Nothing is too small. Use this for anything that didn't fit earlier.

closing_body: Please return this to your Blink strategist at least 48 hours before your Discovery session. Your answers — however rough — give us exactly what we need to make the session as focused and valuable as possible.
